mechanism_narrative: Sustained month-over-month growth in nonfarm payrolls (PAYEMS) reflects a robust economic expansion, leading to higher corporate earnings expectations and positive equity returns over the medium term.
evidence_refs: fact:Recent PAYEMS prints show continued job growth in early 2017.
failure_condition: The rank information coefficient between 3-month changes in PAYEMS and 60-day forward returns is consistently below 0.03 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: When the effective federal funds rate (FEDFUNDS) rises above a key threshold after a prolonged period of accommodation, it signals a committed tightening cycle, increasing discount rates and causing negative equity returns.
evidence_refs: fact:The Federal Reserve hiked rates in December 2016, pushing FEDFUNDS to 0.65%.
failure_condition: S&P 500 forward 20-day returns are positive in more than 60% of instances in the six months following the trigger condition being met.

mechanism_narrative: Rapidly accelerating retail sales (RSAFS) combined with rising inflation (CPIAUCSL) signals an overheating economy, prompting expectations of aggressive monetary tightening by the Federal Reserve, which negatively impacts equity valuations.
candidate_trigger: 3mo % change in RSAFS > 1.5% AND 3mo % change in CPIAUCSL > 0.8%
failure_condition: The dual-trigger condition is not followed by negative S&P 500 returns on average over the subsequent 60 trading days across a full business cycle.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp acceleration in industrial production (INDPRO) during a period of low unemployment indicates the economy is running against capacity constraints, leading to fears of inflation and monetary tightening, thus depressing equity prices.
failure_condition: When UNRATE is below 5.0%, periods of strong INDPRO growth (3-mo change > 1.0) are followed by positive, not negative, 20-day S&P 500 returns on average.

mechanism_narrative: An increase in the unemployment rate (UNRATE) of 0.3 percentage points or more from its 12-month low signals a significant deterioration in the labor market, portending an economic downturn and causing negative equity returns.
failure_condition: In historical backtests, this trigger fails to precede negative 60-day S&P 500 returns in at least two of the last four NBER-defined recessions.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating year-over-year consumer price inflation (CPIAUCSL) erodes the real value of future corporate earnings and increases the probability of restrictive monetary policy, leading to lower equity market valuations.
evidence_refs: fact:Year-over-year CPI has been trending up through 2016 into 2017.
failure_condition: The correlation between the 12-month change in CPIAUCSL and 20-day forward S&P 500 returns is positive over any 5-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Growth in housing starts (HOUST) is a leading indicator of broad economic activity and consumer confidence, signaling future strength in corporate earnings and driving positive equity returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: The 6-month change in HOUST has a statistically insignificant (p > 0.10) or negative relationship with 60-day forward S&P 500 returns over the full sample period.

mechanism_narrative: In a low unemployment environment, an upside surprise in inflation (CPIAUCSL) forces the Federal Reserve to react quickly to maintain its price stability mandate, causing a rapid negative repricing in equity markets.
evidence_refs: fact:UNRATE at 4.8% is below most contemporary estimates of NAIRU.
failure_condition: When UNRATE is below 5.0%, positive inflation surprises (monthly CPI change > 0.3%) are not followed by negative 5-day S&P 500 returns on average.
